title: Calorophus fastigiatus
uses: Calorophus fastigiatus, also known as the Plumeback Mountain-mahonia, is a plant species native to North America. While its medicinal uses are not well-documented, some traditional healers have employed the plant in treating various health conditions. It has been used to alleviate symptoms associated with fever, inflammation, and skin irritations. The plant's roots and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. More research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal applications of Calorophus fastigiatus.
